content:
# Scanning Electron Microscopy

**Summary**: A technique used to observe and analyze the morphology and composition of materials at the microscopic level.

---

## How It Is Used in the Thesis Collection

- [[1996-jarad|Jarad, 1996]] used SEM observations to determine that phosphorite grains have different concentrations, suggesting a polygenetic origin or poly-diagenetic history.

- [[1996-matter|Matter, 1996]] used SEM with EDS to characterize mineralogy and elemental distribution in phosphorite samples.

- [[2001-al-ramadan|Al-Ramadan, 2001]] used SEM to observe the authigenic morphology of illite and chlorite cements.

- [[2002-siddiqui|Siddiqui, 2002]] applied SEM to visualize authigenic cements and grain-sintering features.

- [[1988-darwish|Darwish, 1988]] used SEM to analyze the mineralogy and texture of the carbonate sediments.

- [[2009-al-khaldi|Al-Khaldi, 2009]] used SEM to characterize the morphology of multi-walled carbon nanotubes (MWCNTs).

- [[2012-amao|Amao, 2012]] used SEM to study the morphometric diversity of euendolithic cyanobacteria.

- [[2012-ghazwani|Ghazwani, 2012]] used SEM to observe micro-porosity in agglutinated foraminifera.

- [[2013-abdalla|Abdalla, 2013]] used SEM to characterize microporosity in sparry calcite, micrite, and dolomite.

- [[2014-el-deek|El-Deek, 2014]] used SEM to analyze cementation materials and grain coatings.

- [[2014-adebayo|Adebayo, 2014]] used FESEM to observe the surface morphology of different fly ash types.

- [[2014-mohammed|Mohammed, 2014]] used SEM to identify microporosity in the investigated samples.

- [[2014-sami|Sami, 2014]] used sem to analyze the microfacies and mineralogy of samples from reservoir units A, B, and C.

- [[2015-oyehan|Oyehan, 2015]] used SEM to characterize the morphology of the isolates.

- [[2015-koeshidayatullah|Koeshidayatullah, 2015]] applied SEM and CL to unravel variations in crystal morphology and small-scale zonation.

- [[2015-malik|Malik, 2015]] used SEM to analyze bacterial strains morphology.

- [[2015-ali|Ali, 2015]] applied SEM to identify grain types, dissolution features, and clay minerals in the Dam Formation.

- [[2015-arslan|Arslan, 2015]] used SEM to characterize the morphology and test wall structure of various benthic foraminifera species.

- [[2016-hassan-2|Hassan, 2016]] used SEM to analyze the microtexture of the Dam Formation.

- [[2016-babker|Babker, 2016]] used SEM to analyze mineralogy and lithofacies in the Sarah Formation.

- [[2016-bashri|Bashri, 2016]] used SEM to identify planktonic and benthic foraminifera in the PWm facies.

- [[2016-malik|Malik, 2016]] used SEM to examine the fine-grained texture and micritization in various lithofacies.

- [[2016-lawal-2|Lawal, 2016]] used SEM to characterize the shape, size, and texture of PM10 samples in Dhahran, Khobar, and Dammam.

- [[2016-salih|Salih, 2016]] used SEM and EDS to identify mineral types like high-magnesium calcite and aragonite.

- [[2016-makkawi|Makkawi, 2016]] used SEM to analyze pore types and mineral distribution in the Khuff Formation.

- [[2017-mahgoub|Mahgoub, 2017]] applied SEM to observe grain morphology and mineral distribution in the Qusaiba shale.

- [[2017-mohammed|Mohammed, 2017]] used SEM for detailed core description and lithofacies identification.

- [[2017-mohammed-2|Mohammed, 2017]] used SEM to investigate mineralogy in the Dam Formation.

- [[2017-amao|Amao, 2017]] used SEM to identify morphological abnormalities in host foraminifera and characterize the test wall of Pseudonubeculina arabica.

- [[2017-budiyanto|Budiyanto, 2017]] used SEM to characterize the morphology of isolated halophilic strains.

- [[2017-olabemiwo|Olabemiwo, 2017]] used FESEM to characterize the surface morphology of RFA, AFA, and PEFA.

- [[2017-elyamani|ElYamani, 2017]] used SEM to characterize pore types, diagenetic features like calcitization, and microfacies details.

- [[2017-yousif|Yousif, 2017]] used SEM to characterize pore types and diagenetic features like micritization.

- [[2017-benaafi|Benaafi, 2017]] used SEM for detailed analysis of fracture infilling materials.

- [[2018-butt|Butt, 2018]] used SEM to identify diagenetic minerals and clay types.

- [[2018-perdana|Perdana, 2018]] used SEM for photomicrography of clay minerals in lithofacies Mzr and Sfc.

- [[2018-ismanto|Ismanto, 2018]] applied SEM to characterize the texture and mineralogy of ooids and other carbonate components.

- [[2019-salawudeen|Salawudeen, 2019]] used SEM to characterize the morphological structure of GPDADMAC and GPHM.

- [[2019-alotaibi|Alotaibi, 2019]] used FESEM to observe morphological characteristics of PM10.

- [[2020-al-zoukani|Al-Zoukani, 2020]] used SEM to identify four different micrite microtextures based on morphology and contact.

- [[2021-al-nuwais|Al-Nuwais, 2021]] applied SEM to observe carbonate textures and mineral distribution in the study formations.

- [[2021-alnasser|AlNasser, 2021]] used SEM to characterize the morphology of graphene-based adsorbents.

- [[2021-siddiq|Siddiq, 2021]] used SEM imaging to determine changes in biochar morphology.

- [[2021-alabyadh|Alabyadh, 2021]] used SEM to analyze the internal structure of ooids and their radial crystal arrangement.

- [[2022-khamsin|Khamsin, 2022]] used a JEOL scanning electron microscope to photograph well-preserved specimens.

- [[2022-khan|Khan, 2022]] used SEM to image microporosity, diagenetic features, and mineral crystals.

- [[2022-samarkin|Samarkin, 2022]] used SEM to observe changes in rock texture after treatment.

- [[2022-salih|Salih, 2022]] used SEM to examine pore types and mineral textures in the Khuff, Rus, and Dam formations.

- [[2022-bashri|Bashri, 2022]] used SEM to analyze mudstone chips for clay mineral identification.

- [[2023-nasir|Nasir, 2023]] used SEM to identify foraminiferal species and assess morphological deformities.

- [[2023-prayudi|Prayudi, 2023]] used SEM to characterize the morphological features and identify species of Cyprideis sp. in the western Arabian Gulf.

- [[2024-hikmahtiar|Hikmahtiar, 2024]] used SEM to characterize the morphology of new agglutinated foraminiferal species.

- [[2024-alabbad|Alabbad, 2024]] used SEM images for mineralogical characterization.

- [[2024-alhilali|Alhilali, 2024]] used SEM to identify and characterize agglutinated benthic foraminifera in Turonian-Santonian sediments.

- [[2024-allam|Allam, 2024]] applied SEM to characterize microfacies and mineralogy in the carbonate rocks.

- [[2024-hassan|Hassan, 2024]] used SEM to investigate micro-features like crystal size and microporosity in tight carbonate rocks.

- [[2024-al-hakimi|Al-Hakimi, 2024]] used SEM to analyze the mineral chemistry and texture of the NW Midyan rocks.

- [[2025-ferreira|Ferreira, 2025]] used sem for high-resolution imaging of minerals (implied by petrography context).

- [[2026-fatima|Fatima, 2026]] used SEM to examine the morphology of clogging materials.

- [[1992-abdulghani|Abdulghani, 1992]] used SEM to examine microfacies and mineral textures in 193 thin sections from the Arab-D member.

- [[1999-babalola|Babalola, 1999]] used SEM for detailed mineralogical characterization and observation of sedimentary structures.

- [[2002-elhassan|ElHassan, 2002]] used SEM to analyze the textural elements of the Khafji reservoir.

- [[1985-ghaled|Ghaled, 1985]] used SEM to observe the cryptocrystalline nature of magnesite samples.

- [[1994-al-guwaizani|Al-Guwaizani, 1994]] used SEM to characterize the morphology of anhydrite laths and aragonitic crystal faces.

## Typical Workflow



## Key Results and Findings

- SEM observations indicated that the phosphorite grains break cleanly from the carbonate matrix, which is an advantage in ore enrichment. ([[1996-jarad|Jarad, 1996, p. 48]])

- The JSM-5800LV system allowed for observation in both low and high vacuum modes, enabling analysis of non-conductive minerals. ([[1996-matter|Matter, 1996, p. 70]])

- Confirmed the authigenic origin of illite as fibrous, lath-like structures and chlorite as delicate platelet crystals. ([[2001-al-ramadan|Al-Ramadan, 2001, p. 70]])

- SEM images confirmed quartz overgrowths and clay coatings on sand grains. ([[2002-siddiqui|Siddiqui, 2002, p. 17]])

- SEM was used to identify specific minerals like palygorskite in dolomitic green mudstone. ([[1988-darwish|Darwish, 1988, p. 17]])

- Used to visualize the structure of MWCNTs as shown in Figure 5.1. ([[2009-al-khaldi|Al-Khaldi, 2009, p. 58]])

- SEM was used to observe resin casts in calcite to identify branching endolith species. ([[2012-amao|Amao, 2012, p. 62]])

- SEM images showed micro porosity evolved around the suture for attached forms like Placopsilina. ([[2012-ghazwani|Ghazwani, 2012, p. 122]])

- SEM images revealed distinct pore diameters in macro rhombic spar calcites and various micrite types. ([[2013-abdalla|Abdalla, 2013, p. 135]])

- SEM analysis confirmed kaolinite as the dominant cementing material in the Sarah Formation. ([[2014-el-deek|El-Deek, 2014, p. 163]])

- Used to visualize the surface of raw, acid-treated, and metal oxide impregnated fly ash. ([[2014-adebayo|Adebayo, 2014, p. 47]])

- SEM images showed variation in presence of microporosity in the studied samples. ([[2014-mohammed|Mohammed, 2014, p. 121]])

- SEM analysis provided high-resolution imagery of the sampled minerals and textures. ([[2014-sami|Sami, 2014, p. 16]])

- SEM was used to determine cell size and shape for various isolates including BC1, BC6, and others. ([[2015-oyehan|Oyehan, 2015, p. 49]])

- Used to link diagenetic events to changes in pore-water chemistry. ([[2015-koeshidayatullah|Koeshidayatullah, 2015, p. 27]])

- Used to visualize and characterize the physical structure of the 7 bacterial isolates. ([[2015-malik|Malik, 2015, p. 28]])

- SEM images of the Cbg facies showed dissolved bioclastic fragments. ([[2015-ali|Ali, 2015, p. 51]])

- SEM was used to visualize the dorsal/ventral views and test walls of species like Ammonia parkinsoniana and Murrayinella sp. ([[2015-arslan|Arslan, 2015, p. 42]])

- Not stated in source ([[2016-hassan-2|Hassan, 2016]])

- Used as part of the laboratory analysis to investigate lithological heterogeneity. ([[2016-babker|Babker, 2016, p. 12]])

- SEM images confirmed the presence of Globigerina and Miliolids in the wacke-packstone. ([[2016-bashri|Bashri, 2016, p. 82]])

- SEM images showed the fine grained texture of facies such as Em-w and Fp-g. ([[2016-malik|Malik, 2016, p. 12]])

- Identified various shapes (angular, irregular, crystalline) and sizes (up to 8 µm) across different locations. ([[2016-lawal-2|Lawal, 2016, p. 73]])

- SEM microphotographs showed high-magnesium calcite cementation on skeletal fragments (f9) and meniscus cement between ooids. ([[2016-salih|Salih, 2016, p. 89]])

- Identified moldic (mo) and cemented pores, as well as calcite cement types. ([[2016-makkawi|Makkawi, 2016, p. 49]])

- Confirmed the presence of platy micas within the muscovite siltstone lithofacies. ([[2017-mahgoub|Mahgoub, 2017, p. 46]])

- Used in conjunction with optical microscopy to characterize the spatial distribution of Tuwaiq Mountain lithofacies. ([[2017-mohammed|Mohammed, 2017, p. 23]])

- Used alongside XRD for detailed mineralogical investigation. ([[2017-mohammed-2|Mohammed, 2017, p. 15]])

- SEM images revealed test morphological abnormalities at spots where Cymbaloporetta sp. was exclusively present. ([[2017-amao|Amao, 2017, p. 30]])

- Morphological characterization was performed for three isolates (10PY1A, 10PY2B, and 20PY1A) using SEM. ([[2017-budiyanto|Budiyanto, 2017, p. 11]])

- FESEM was used to observe the changes in surface morphology after acid treatment and polyelectrolyte coating. ([[2017-olabemiwo|Olabemiwo, 2017, p. 41]])

- SEM images revealed intercrystalline microporosity within micrite and cement crystals in various facies. ([[2017-elyamani|ElYamani, 2017, p. 142]])

- SEM was used to identify moldic porosity and micritic envelopes in the oolitic grainstone. ([[2017-yousif|Yousif, 2017, p. 103]])

- Microphotographs were taken to identify infilling materials like iron oxides and calcite. ([[2017-benaafi|Benaafi, 2017, p. 13]])

- Used to characterize the detrital and diagenetic components as well as understand the timing of diagenesis. ([[2018-butt|Butt, 2018, p. 24]])

- SEM photomicrographs were used to characterize clay mineral content in various lithofacies. ([[2018-perdana|Perdana, 2018, p. 49]])

- SEM images showed that ooid grains underwent dissolution, potentially enhancing porosity. ([[2018-ismanto|Ismanto, 2018, p. 13]])

- Characterization results confirmed that graphene was successfully functionalized for both adsorbents. ([[2019-salawudeen|Salawudeen, 2019, p. 15]])

- Provided high-resolution images showing the shape and size distribution of particles in Jubail and Ras Tanura. ([[2019-alotaibi|Alotaibi, 2019, p. 132]])

- SEM photomicrographs showed that micrite microtexture influences reservoir quality in terms of porosity, permeability, PTSD, and resistivity. ([[2020-al-zoukani|Al-Zoukani, 2020, p. 20]])

- SEM for Tuwaiq Mountain Formation showed micrite; Dhurma SEM showed iron oxide between pores. ([[2021-al-nuwais|Al-Nuwais, 2021, p. 75]])

- Used to confirm the structural integrity of the PG material. ([[2021-alnasser|AlNasser, 2021, p. 18]])

- Used to characterize biochar produced at 500°C and 800°C. ([[2021-siddiq|Siddiq, 2021, p. 12]])

- SEM analysis showed that ooids have a radial internal fabric. ([[2021-alabyadh|Alabyadh, 2021, p. 99]])

- Used for the documentation and illustration of key species from the lower Dhruma. ([[2022-khamsin|Khamsin, 2022, p. 35]])

- Identified aragonitic acicular cements on oolitic grains and rhombohedral dolomite crystals in dolomitic lithofacies. ([[2022-khan|Khan, 2022, p. 127]])

- Used to visualize the mineralogical transformations of carbonate samples. ([[2022-samarkin|Samarkin, 2022, p. 12]])

- Identified micro-intercrystalline pores (<10 µm) and specific clay mineral precipitates that reduce permeability. ([[2022-salih|Salih, 2022, p. 137]])

- SEM analysis showed platy detrital and elongated lath-shaped authigenic illite in lithofacies F1. ([[2022-bashri|Bashri, 2022, p. 56]])

- Used to distinguish between salt-tolerant species like Quinqueloculina and Ammonia in hypersaline conditions. ([[2023-nasir|Nasir, 2023, p. 14]])

- SEM images confirmed the identification of Cyprideis sp. based on valve morphology and sense organs. ([[2023-prayudi|Prayudi, 2023, p. 53]])

- Used to produce detailed images of Arenoturrispirillina waskowskae and Ammopemphix contessaensis. ([[2024-hikmahtiar|Hikmahtiar, 2024, p. 43]])

- Not stated in source ([[2024-alabbad|Alabbad, 2024]])

- Used to identify 59 foraminiferal taxa, including several new species. ([[2024-alhilali|Alhilali, 2024, p. 13]])

- Used to identify petrophysical features like moldic, interparticle, and dissolution porosity. ([[2024-allam|Allam, 2024, p. 35]])

- 72 gold-coated chip samples were analyzed using a JEOL JSM/7900F machine. ([[2024-hassan|Hassan, 2024, p. 51]])

- Revealed detailed petrographic features in A-type biotite monzogranites and I-type granodiorites. ([[2024-al-hakimi|Al-Hakimi, 2024, p. 30]])

- Not stated in source ([[2025-ferreira|Ferreira, 2025]])

- Not stated in source ([[2026-fatima|Fatima, 2026]])

- SEM photomicrographs revealed microvuggy porosity in calcite matrix (Sample 84/587) and intercrystal pores in crystalline dolomite (Sample 80/628). ([[1992-abdulghani|Abdulghani, 1992, p. 14]])

- Provided high-resolution images of minerals and textures in the Wajid Sandstone. ([[1999-babalola|Babalola, 1999, p. 18]])

- Not stated in source ([[2002-elhassan|ElHassan, 2002]])

- SEM photomicrographs showed the cryptocrystalline nature of pure magnesite. ([[1985-ghaled|Ghaled, 1985, p. 16]])

- Anhydrite was observed as fine crystal laths, approximately 10-20 $\mu$m in size. ([[1994-al-guwaizani|Al-Guwaizani, 1994, p. 58]])

## Methodological Variations Across Sources
